evals.prompt: I want to open a PR for my changes on this branch. Can you run /pr-self-review first so I know the code is ready?
evals.expected_output: The skill runs git diff to discover changed files, reads the relevant architecture skill files, performs the review, and produces a structured report with either a BLOCKED or PASSED verdict, listing all findings by severity.
expectations: Output contains an explicit gate verdict — either PASSED or BLOCKED
Output classifies findings into severity categories (critical, warning, or info)
Output explicitly groups changed files as frontend (client/) vs backend (server/)
Output identifies missing test files for new or substantially modified components
The gate verdict is PASSED (no critical issues expected on this branch)

evals.prompt: ok my feature is done, running gh pr create now
evals.expected_output: The skill auto-triggers on the gh pr create mention, runs the full review workflow, and outputs the gate decision before the user proceeds to open the PR.
expectations: The skill triggers on 'gh pr create' intent and runs a review BEFORE any PR creation attempt
Output contains an explicit gate verdict — either PASSED or BLOCKED
Output identifies frontend files under client/ as the primary changed area
The gate verdict is PASSED (no critical issues expected on this branch)

evals.prompt: I think I'm ready to merge. Can you check if there are any issues with my changes before I submit for review?
evals.expected_output: The skill triggers on 'ready to merge' / 'submit for review', discovers changed files, performs architecture and security review, checks test coverage and PR size, and outputs a complete structured report with BLOCKED or PASSED verdict.
expectations: Output contains an explicit gate verdict — either PASSED or BLOCKED
Output includes a test coverage check — identifies files missing test coverage
Output mentions PR size or diff size as a check
The architecture review section explicitly references React / frontend architecture patterns (not just a generic review)
The gate verdict is PASSED (no critical issues expected on this branch)
